frameworks.name: Bayesian cue combination
frameworks.one_line: memory uncertainty is integrated with priors and rewards to guide decisions

frameworks.name: working memory with trial-to-trial uncertainty
frameworks.one_line: WM representations carry graded reliability accessible for downstream choice

frameworks.name: rewarded decision-making
frameworks.one_line: subjects use elicited uncertainty to maximize reward in choice tasks

core_question: Do people incorporate trial-to-trial working memory uncertainty into rewarded decisions and combine it with prior beliefs?
key_assumptions: working memory carries graded, trial-varying uncertainty
subjects can report memory uncertainty via rewarded choices
uncertainty can be combined with priors via Bayesian-like integration
manipulations: color stimuli held in working memory
induced prior expectations over stimulus colors
key_findings: reported memory uncertainty correlates with memory error
prior-induced shift increases with reported uncertainty
interpretations: people access and use trial-to-trial WM uncertainty in decisions
memory uncertainty is combined with rewards and prior beliefs
